title: High Bandwidth Utilization / Traffic Congestion
symptoms: Interface utilization exceeds 80% sustained for >5 minutes
QoS queues dropping packets for high-priority traffic
Customers reporting slow speeds or latency
SNMP threshold alarm from NMS for ifInOctets/ifOutOctets
steps: Identify the congested interface and direction (in/out): 'show interfaces <intf>' — check input/output rates
Use NetFlow or IPSLA to identify top talkers: which source/destination pairs are consuming bandwidth
Check if this is a sudden spike (DDoS, backup job, video broadcast) or a sustained growth trend
Verify QoS policy is applied and working: 'show policy-map interface <intf>'
Check if a backup or large transfer job is scheduled — confirm with the application/server team
For DDoS suspicion: check for single-source floods or amplification patterns (UDP, ICMP)
If peering link: check ECMP load balancing — traffic may be unequally distributed
Review historical utilization in NMS to determine if this is a capacity planning issue
resolution: Apply rate-limiting for identified bulk flows. For DDoS: null-route the target or upstream scrubbing center. Reroute traffic to a less-congested path if available. For capacity: request bandwidth upgrade from ISP/transport provider. Adjust QoS policy to protect critical traffic during congestion.
escalation: If congestion is caused by a DDoS attack, escalate to Security team and activate DDoS mitigation SOP. If congestion affects SLA-bound customer circuits, notify Account Management and open urgent capacity ticket.
estimated_resolution_time: Traffic shaping: 10 minutes. DDoS mitigation: 30-60 minutes. Capacity upgrade: days/weeks.
reference: Emircom Capacity & Traffic Management SOP — NET-CAP-006
